14623.leader: Analyze problems to develop solutions involving computer hardware and software.

14626.leader: Apply theoretical expertise and innovation to create or apply new technology, such as adapting principles for applying computers to new uses.

14624.leader: Assign or schedule tasks to meet work priorities and goals.

14628.leader: Meet with managers, vendors, and others to solicit cooperation and resolve problems.

14633.leader: Design computers and the software that runs them.

14629.leader: Conduct logical analyses of business, scientific, engineering, and other technical problems, formulating mathematical models of problems for solution by computers.

14625.leader: Evaluate project plans and proposals to assess feasibility issues.

14635.leader: Participate in multidisciplinary projects in areas such as virtual reality, human-computer interaction, or robotics.

14627.leader: Consult with users, management, vendors, and technicians to determine computing needs and system requirements.

14630.leader: Develop and interpret organizational goals, policies, and procedures.

14632.leader: Develop performance standards, and evaluate work in light of established standards.

a579b808-d5ff-4ea0-9bb5-196d8299198c.text: Review research literature to identify relevant prior work and techniques.

source_statements: Read current state-of-the-art research papers in AI and climate science to identify new techniques and ideas for improving climate prediction.
Sift through large volumes of new literature in the vision-language model (VLM) space to identify relevant papers for research.
Conduct independent research, including implementation, literature review, and brainstorming new ideas, using AI tools to increase productivity.
Use AI tools to conduct deep research before meetings with advisors to discuss prior works and assess feasibility of proposed research topics.
metadata.notes: Merged literature-scanning and prior-work review tasks across AI/climate and VLM contexts. AI tools were explicitly mentioned by one participant and can generalize to the broader review activity.

statement_parts.outcome: to identify relevant prior work and techniques

f6002d08-24c7-419c-a8e5-7193abdcdb31.text: Compare and select AI methods for identified research problems to choose approaches for experimentation.

source_statements: Survey, compare, and select AI techniques for adaptation and application to identified climate science problems.
metadata.notes: Distinct from literature review because it focuses on evaluating candidate methods for a specific problem and deciding what to test.

statement_parts.object: AI methods for identified research problems

d5b58853-1ec2-4603-9dae-0f4bc2d7e863.text: Consult domain experts to identify research problems that can be addressed with AI methods.

source_statements: Collaborate and communicate with climate science experts to identify key problems and challenges in the field that can be addressed using AI techniques.
metadata.notes: Kept separate because it centers on eliciting problem definitions from experts rather than reviewing literature or selecting methods.

statement_parts.outcome: to identify research problems that can be addressed with AI methods

728c0778-7c2b-4acf-a166-379b4b3f1235.text: Design experiments and prototype model architectures to test research hypotheses and evaluate AI methods.

source_statements: Conduct experiments with selected AI methods, including coding and debugging, to apply them to climate science problems and evaluate their effectiveness.
Design experiments and explore new NLP architectures to ensure technical rigor in model evaluation frameworks.
Brainstorm and prototype new architectures for multimodal alignment or novel evaluation metrics to validate research hypotheses.
metadata.notes: Merged experiment design, execution, and architecture prototyping because all describe setting up and running research tests. Preserved both hypothesis-testing and evaluation aspects.

statement_parts.outcome: to test research hypotheses and evaluate AI methods

81d93785-4d37-4289-bbd5-9e88879c82da.text: Write code for model training, inference, and research prototypes to produce runnable experimental systems.

source_statements: Conduct experiments with selected AI methods, including coding and debugging, to apply them to climate science problems and evaluate their effectiveness.
Develop and run code for experiments, model inference, and training scripts to translate theoretical NLP concepts into tangible results.
Offload repetitive boilerplate coding, data preparation, and verification tasks to AI tools to reduce tedium and focus on research.
Perform coding tasks to solve problems in the context of AI research and product development.
metadata.notes: Merged coding tasks tied to AI research and experimentation. Included AI-assisted boilerplate coding as a variant of the same core activity.

statement_parts.object: code for model training, inference, and research prototypes

d253b7f1-f556-4227-9378-f0e995b0c4b8.text: Debug research code and distributed systems to resolve failures in experimental and production workflows.

source_statements: Conduct experiments with selected AI methods, including coding and debugging, to apply them to climate science problems and evaluate their effectiveness.
Debug issues in large-scale distributed systems to ensure reliability and efficiency.
metadata.notes: Merged debugging activities because both involve diagnosing and fixing technical issues, while preserving that one occurs in research code and the other in distributed systems.

statement_parts.outcome: to resolve failures in experimental and production workflows

aad836a4-042b-4d5e-8038-fcd46b85a7f5.text: Analyze model outputs and platform data to identify patterns, biases, and decision-relevant findings.

source_statements: Analyze model outputs to identify patterns or biases that inform evaluation frameworks.
Perform data analysis on the peer review platform to support decision-making and improve platform outcomes.
Use AI tools to explore data, prototype solutions, and understand complex system behavior to work more efficiently and focus on higher-level problem solving.
metadata.notes: Merged analysis of model outputs, operational platform data, and AI-assisted data exploration because all involve examining data to extract actionable findings.

statement_parts.outcome: to identify patterns, biases, and decision-relevant findings

b36670da-d4c1-46ad-b48f-69e9650a3435.text: Annotate datasets to create ground-truth labels for model evaluation.

source_statements: Handle data annotation workflows to ensure high-quality ground truth data for robust evaluations.
metadata.notes: Distinct manual data-preparation task requiring labeling rather than analysis or coding.

statement_parts.outcome: to create ground-truth labels for model evaluation

681a20b3-503b-47eb-9411-522d3a0d783e.text: Organize experimental results, model checkpoints, and configuration files to track ablation studies.

source_statements: Organize and manage experimental results, model checkpoints, and configuration files during ablation studies to facilitate actionable insights.
metadata.notes: Kept separate because it is specifically about experiment artifact tracking with a named tool.

statement_parts.object: experimental results, model checkpoints, and configuration files

ecb7d206-056b-45cd-bafd-9b1a96e5eb81.text: Write research findings and experimental results in papers or documentation to record and communicate outcomes.

source_statements: Write up research findings and results from experiments for documentation or publication.
metadata.notes: Distinct dissemination task focused on producing written research outputs.

statement_parts.object: research findings and experimental results in papers or documentation

d0f9d8e0-5da7-4184-8b9d-9450e64a2d01.text: Write code for large-scale distributed systems to deliver scalable and reliable system functionality.

source_statements: Write and review code for distributed systems to improve scalability and reliability.
metadata.notes: Kept separate from research coding because it targets distributed systems engineering rather than model experimentation.

statement_parts.outcome: to deliver scalable and reliable system functionality

1b7a46a5-9d39-463f-8ad8-10855f279438.text: Break down ambiguous technical problems into structured components to define implementation and design steps.

source_statements: Break down ambiguous or messy technical problems into structured components to enable problem-solving and system design.
metadata.notes: Distinct analytical planning task that precedes coding or experimentation.

statement_parts.object: ambiguous technical problems into structured components
statement_parts.outcome: to define implementation and design steps

224eaca1-b822-444d-809b-d051d030a94a.text: Prototype natural-language interfaces for experiment data to enable users to query results in plain language.

source_statements: Prototype AI-assisted interfaces for querying experiment data in natural language to reduce user friction and speed up data access.
metadata.notes: Separate interface prototyping task with a specific user-facing artifact.

statement_parts.outcome: to enable users to query results in plain language

f0a2b4b1-442b-4006-9906-777a93af45f2.text: Develop large language models and training or inference methods to produce new AI capabilities.

source_statements: Develop new methods in large language model (LLM) training and inference, with a special focus on agentic workflow, to drive AI innovation.
Serve as a research associate to help build a large language model (LLM) for a university in the UAE.
metadata.notes: Merged building an LLM with developing new LLM training/inference methods because both center on creating LLM systems, though one emphasizes methodological innovation.

statement_parts.object: large language models and training or inference methods

034728df-e3f6-4f49-b00a-4beb9ba635ff.text: Design agentic workflows for AI systems to make them reusable across applications.

source_statements: Design agentic workflows to ensure they can be scaled up easily across different applications.
metadata.notes: Kept separate from LLM development and agent implementation because it focuses on workflow architecture.

statement_parts.outcome: to make them reusable across applications

b2e9408b-0d8a-4359-9945-ec4520000fa3.text: Build conversational agents to deliver agent-based interactions.

source_statements: Use agentic workflow to build conversation agents using a proprietary framework.
metadata.notes: Distinct implementation task for a specific class of AI application.

a4b72fa2-2441-4789-ba22-7802267c5106.text: Review pull requests to identify where AI assistance can be added to the development workflow.

source_statements: Review pull requests (PR reviews), a repetitive and less enjoyable task, and identify opportunities where AI assistance could improve manageability.
metadata.notes: Separate code-review task focused on process improvement opportunities.

statement_parts.outcome: to identify where AI assistance can be added to the development workflow

59cea311-559b-4567-9067-1d578af62032.text: Coordinate peer review platform workflows to keep review operations running smoothly.

source_statements: Manage workflows for a peer review platform to ensure smooth operation and support for an NLP organization.
metadata.notes: Distinct operational coordination task tied to a platform rather than research or coding.

statement_parts.outcome: to keep review operations running smoothly

a631256b-7a3e-4713-b899-b94fa998743c.text: Resolve user issues on the peer review platform to restore platform functionality.

source_statements: Provide technical support for the peer review platform to resolve user issues and maintain platform functionality.
metadata.notes: Kept separate because troubleshooting user-reported platform issues is different from workflow coordination or data analysis.

statement_parts.object: user issues on the peer review platform

criteria: Same core action and object domain (e.g. both involve reviewing code with teammates) OR
One is a specific instance of the other (attending standups IS sharing progress updates with the team) OR
One is a subtask of the other
A job analyst would describe them with the same canonical task statement
Tasks represent the same type of work even if phrased differently by different people
